content: Air (density = 1.2 kg/m<sup>3</sup>, kinematic viscosity = 1.5 × 10<sup>-5</sup> m<sup>2</sup>/s) flows over a flat plate with a free-stream velocity of 2 m/s. The wall shear stress at a location 15 mm from the leading edge is τ<sub>w</sub>. What is the wall shear stress at a location 30 mm from the leading edge?

options.content: √2τw
2 τw

explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & \operatorname{Re}=\frac{u_{\infty} L}{v}=\frac{2 \times 0.03}{1.5 \times 10^{-5}} \\\\ & \operatorname{Re}=4000<5 \times 10^5 \\\\ & \frac{\tau_{w_2}}{\tau_{w_1}}=\left(\frac{x_1}{x_2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}=\left(\frac{15}{30}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} \\\\ & \tau_{w_2}=\frac{\tau_{w_1}}{\sqrt{2}} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (D).

content: Within a boundary layer for a steady incompressible flow, the Bernoulli equation

options.content: holds because the flow is steady
holds because the flow is incompressible
holds because the flow is transitional
does not hold because the flow is frictional

options.content: A reduction of pressure to vapour pressure
The boundary layer thickness reducing to zero

content:
The necessary and sufficient condition which brings about separation of boundary layer is
$${{dP} \over {dx}} > 0$$

options.content: The flow is along the streamlines
